not_started: Before I trust you with bigger work, speak to Mira at the market stall for me.
Return after you've checked in with her.
in_progress: Find her by the market stall and then report back to me.
ready_to_turn_in: That tells me you can listen, explore, and return with answers.
Your first village errand is complete.

lines: You've already proven yourself to me.
Would you like me to prepare your next assignment?

response_lines: Good. Then listen carefully.
Read the village sign, recover Mira's supply ledger from the sealed chest, and bring it to her.
Fair enough.
Take a breath and come back when you're ready.

quest_two_visit_sign: Your supply run is active now.
Read the village sign in the square, then head east to the sealed supply chest for Mira's ledger.
quest_two_open_chest: Good. You've read the sign and confirmed the route.
Now open the sealed supply chest near the eastern house and bring Mira's ledger to the market.
quest_two_report_to_mira: Take it to Mira at the market stall so she can finish the supply check.
quest_two_complete: You handled the village supply run well.
That kind of follow-through is exactly what Oakcross needs.

not_started: If Elder Rowan sent you, come back after you've spoken with him first.
in_progress: Tell him the market is ready and the village is counting on him.
ready_to_turn_in: You've already got what you need from me.
Rowan is waiting for your report.

response_lines: Mostly bread, herbs, and a few travel tools.
Take a look. If your purse can handle it, the stock is yours.
No pressure.
Come back when your pack feels a little too empty.

lines: You kept the route, the chest, and the delivery straight.
Take these healer's herbs as thanks. That completes the supply run.
If Rowan sends you again, you'll be ready.
Need anything from the market while you're here?

response_lines: Mostly bread, herbs, and a few travel tools.
Take a look. If your purse can handle it, the stock is yours.
No pressure.
Come back when your pack feels a little too empty.

lines: The south road has turned ugly. Rats are nesting in the tall grass, chewing sacks, and scaring off anyone headed for the lower fields.
If you can clear them out, bring me 10 Rat Tails as proof.

response_lines: Thank you.
Follow the road south from the square. Don't just fight them, remember to pick up the tails.
Bring me 10 and I'll know the infestation is handled.
I understand.
But if the scratching gets any closer to the stalls, I might climb onto the roof and stay there.

lines: The rats are still thick south of the village.
Bring me 10 Rat Tails so I know the road is safe again.
You have the tails? Good. That means the south road can breathe again.
Hand them over and I'll make it worth the trouble.

response_lines: Perfect. That's exactly what I needed.
Oakcross owes you for keeping the road clear. Take this for your work.
Your pack, your choice.
Bring them back when you're ready to finish the job.

lines: The south road is calmer now.
If the rats come back, I'll know who to ask.

response_lines: Mostly bread, herbs, and a few travel tools.
Take a look. If your purse can handle it, the stock is yours.
No pressure.
Come back when your pack feels a little too empty.
